title: [Bug]: RetryPolicy direct construction accepts negative backoff values
summary: The RetryPolicy dataclass accepts negative backoff timing when constructed directly through the Python API.
observed: A direct RetryPolicy instance can contain negative backoff values even though runtime configuration bounds them.
expected: RetryPolicy must reject negative backoff timing at construction time.
reproduction: Run the focused regression test that constructs RetryPolicy with a negative base backoff.
impact: Embedding applications could produce confusing retry delays if they construct policies directly.
delivery_semantics: The fix preserves bounded retry semantics and does not alter ACK ordering.
security: Explicit bounds avoid hidden timing behavior in failure handling.
acceptance: RetryPolicy rejects negative base backoff values.
RetryPolicy rejects negative maximum backoff values.
Valid fixed, linear, and exponential policies continue to work.
The focused regression test and full checks pass.
tests: Run the focused regression test, retry tests, and the full local check script.
documentation: Update retry documentation or release notes if public RetryPolicy validation is clarified.
closeout: Close only after the release containing the fix is published with regression and verification evidence.
